evidence_quotes.quote: bad actors in the gaming industry from permanently destroying or disabling products that consumers have paid for.

paragraphs.text: Living in the EU has many benefits, such as the ability to work and live abroad in other EU countries without complications. However, what makes me most proud to be an EU citizen is our commitment to consumer rights. I strongly urge the European Union to include provisions in the Digital Fairness Act that prevent bad actors in the gaming industry from permanently destroying or disabling products that consumers have paid for. Many artists and developers pour their hearts and souls into creating these pieces of digital art for us to enjoy. It is disheartening to see companies destroy these creations out of fear that consumers will buy fewer copies of their new, often inferior titles. This practice effectively exploits consumers and forces them to purchase uninspired "products" without any real choice. Thank you for considering our voices in this important matter.
